shelves.label: Clear workhorses
shelves.description: Calm families that carry argument, evidence and handoff without becoming wallpaper.
shelves.oneThingNotToDo: Do not confuse neutrality with an excuse to make every slide identical.

shelves.label: Warm humanists
shelves.description: Open forms and human rhythm for decks that need warmth without performing friendliness.
shelves.oneThingNotToDo: Do not round every corner until the argument loses its spine.

shelves.label: Geometric voices
shelves.description: Designed presence for titles that should be remembered without shouting at the body copy.
shelves.oneThingNotToDo: Do not let the display face colonize paragraphs, labels and footnotes.

shelves.label: Condensed impact
shelves.description: Space-efficient display voices for covers, chapter cards and large-room statements.
shelves.oneThingNotToDo: Do not use condensed width to preserve copy that should have been edited.

shelves.label: Editorial reading
shelves.description: Serifs with enough stamina for treatments, narrative decks and argument that needs pace.
shelves.oneThingNotToDo: Do not use literary texture as permission for paragraphs that should be slides apart.

shelves.label: Expressive serif
shelves.description: Display faces with texture, contrast and an actual opinion.
shelves.oneThingNotToDo: Do not turn every sentence into a costume change.

shelves.label: Sturdy serifs
shelves.description: Serifs that can hold numbers, product detail and long argument without becoming polite wallpaper.
shelves.oneThingNotToDo: Do not mistake weight for authority; hierarchy still has to do the work.

shelves.label: Data and utility
shelves.description: Monospaced companions for numbers, evidence, labels and technical material—not a cyberpunk personality transplant.
shelves.oneThingNotToDo: Do not dress ordinary prose as code to make the deck look technical.
